prompt: You've given me a clear target: keep both border lights locked together as one calm, same-speed glow (pulse leading the pretty edge-glow), maybe dropping the pulse later. Here's how we could work the problem — pick a method and I'll run it.

cards.label: Option Funnel
cards.blurb: Diverge a spread of ways they could relate, then narrow to the winner.
cards.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#3b2141"/><g stroke="#a855f7" stroke-width="1" fill="none" opacity="0.4"><path d="M20 24 L50 78"/><path d="M35 22 L50 78"/><path d="M50 21 L50 78"/><path d="M65 22 L50 78"/><path d="M80 24 L50 78"/></g><g fill="#f0efeb"><circle cx="20" cy="24" r="2.4"/><circle cx="35" cy="22" r="2.4"/><circle cx="50" cy="21" r="2.4"/><circle cx="65" cy="22" r="2.4"/><circle cx="80" cy="24" r="2.4"/></g><circle cx="50" cy="80" r="12" fill="#a855f7" opacity="0.3"/><circle cx="50" cy="80" r="7" fill="#e75af2" opacity="0.7"/><circle cx="50" cy="80" r="3.5" fill="#ffffff"/></svg>
cards.reason: You said you want 'one unified light' and to 'keep both locked together' — the funnel is exactly that: I'll diverge a handful of ways the pulse and edge-glow can lock into one calm same-speed light, you judge and narrow, and we crown the one that feels right (with 'remove the pulse later' still on the table).

cards.label: Mind Map
cards.blurb: Co-arrange the facets of the relationship on one live tree if the shape is still forming.
cards.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#3b2141"/><g stroke="#a855f7" stroke-width="1" fill="none" opacity="0.45"><path d="M50 50 L22 22"/><path d="M50 50 L80 24"/><path d="M50 50 L24 78"/><path d="M50 50 L82 76"/></g><g fill="#f0efeb"><circle cx="22" cy="22" r="3"/><circle cx="80" cy="24" r="3"/><circle cx="24" cy="78" r="3"/><circle cx="82" cy="76" r="3"/></g><circle cx="50" cy="50" r="11" fill="#a855f7" opacity="0.28"/><circle cx="46" cy="50" r="4.5" fill="#e75af2" opacity="0.85"/><circle cx="54" cy="50" r="4.5" fill="#ffffff"/></svg>

cards.label: Wreck It
cards.blurb: Stress-test the 'lock them together' idea — where would it break or look wrong?
cards.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#3b2141"/><g stroke="#a855f7" stroke-width="1.4" fill="none" opacity="0.55"><path d="M46 20.3 A30 30 0 0 0 20 54"/><path d="M54 79.7 A30 30 0 0 0 80 46"/></g><circle cx="20" cy="54" r="3.2" fill="#f0efeb"/><circle cx="80" cy="46" r="3.2" fill="#f0efeb"/><path d="M55 12 L44 42 L54 48 L42 88" stroke="#e75af2" stroke-width="2.4" fill="none" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg>

cards.label: Cluster
cards.blurb: Drop many relationship ideas on a field and group them by nearness.
cards.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#3b2141"/><ellipse cx="30" cy="36" rx="16" ry="13" fill="#a855f7" opacity="0.14"/><ellipse cx="72" cy="68" rx="15" ry="12" fill="#a855f7" opacity="0.14"/><g fill="#f0efeb"><circle cx="24" cy="32" r="2.6"/><circle cx="34" cy="30" r="2.6"/><circle cx="30" cy="42" r="2.6"/></g><g fill="#e75af2"><circle cx="68" cy="64" r="2.6"/><circle cx="78" cy="66" r="2.6"/><circle cx="73" cy="76" r="2.6"/></g></svg>
